zh.Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾圖福德
zh.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol於大約1107年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾圖福德。他於大約1130年與Mary Jordan在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾圖福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他於1130年住在英國諾廷加姆爾。他去世於從1141年到1147年在法國諾門迪。

zh-hans.Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, England: 英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾圖福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol于大约1107年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾圖福德。他于大约1130年与Mary Jordan在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾圖福德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他于1130年住在英国諾廷加姆爾。他去世于从1141年到1147年在法国諾門迪。

nameMeanings.name: fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: (i) relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + roy ‘king’, ‘son of the king’. It was often taken to imply that the original bearer was a bastard son of the king ( compare Fillary ). (ii) relationship name ‘son of Ruadh’, a Gaelic nickname meaning ‘red’, is also possible.

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Alao
meanings: West African (Nigeria): unexplained.
Filipino: metonymic occupational name from a Hispanicized form of Ilokano alaw, a term denoting a method used to get back a lost spirit.
In some cases possibly also Portuguese (Alão): from the personal name Alão, an equivalent of Alan .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Jordan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a name given to a child of either sex baptized in holy water that was, purportedly at least, brought from the River Jordan, whose Hebrew name, ha-yarden, means ‘flowing down’. It was in this river that Christ was baptized by John the Baptist, and medieval pilgrims to the Holy Land usually tried to bring back a flask of its water with them. The modern given name is either a revival of this, or else a transferred use of the surname that was derived from the medieval given name. It is more popular as a boy's name in Britain and as a girl's name in the United States.

It was in this river that Christ was baptized by John the Baptist, and medieval pilgrims to the Holy Land usually tried to bring back a flask of its water with them. en.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol was born about 1107, in Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, England. He married Mary Jordan about 1130, in Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He lived in Nottinghamshire, England in 1130. He died from 1141 to 1147, in Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol est né environ 1107, à Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Mary Jordan environ 1130, à Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il a vécu à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre en 1130. Il est décédé du 1141 au 1147, à Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol wurde um 1107 in Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, England geboren. Er hat um 1130 in Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Mary Jordan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. 1130 hat er in Nottinghamshire, England gelebt. Er starb von 1141 bis 1147 in Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol è nato il circa 1107 nel Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, England. Lui sposato Mary Jordan circa 1130, nel Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Vissuto a Nottinghamshire, England nel 1130. Morì da 1141 a 1147, nel Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dolは約1107年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアトゥクスフォードで生まれた。約1130年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアトゥクスフォードでMary Jordanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1130年にはイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアに住んでいた。彼はから1141年まで1147年にフランスノーマンディで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol(은)는 대략 1107년 영국 노틴그함실르 투스퍼드에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1130년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 투스퍼드에서 Mary Jordan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1130년도에 영국 노틴그함실르에서 살았다. 그는 1141년 부터 1147년 까지 프랑스 노먼디에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol nasceu aproximadamente 1107, em Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mary Jordan aproximadamente 1130, em Tuxford, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele viveu em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra em 1130. Ele faleceu de 1141 a 1147, em Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Jordan fitz Alan I Fifth Steward of Dol родился около 1107 г. в местности туксфорд, ноттингхамшир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Jordan около 1130 г. в местности туксфорд, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он жил в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия в 1130 году. Он умер с 1141 по 1147 в местности норманди, Франция.
